programme.title: Capítulo 30
programme.desc: Mia tries to kiss Miguel but gets cold feet. Alma asks Roberta what she's been spending so much money on. Both Roberta and Mia like the new student.

programme.title: Capítulo 31
programme.desc: Mia's not allowed out and so her date's ruined. Miguel gets Celina to understand they have to split. Roberta has a plan to get out of Diego's grip.

programme.title: Capítulo 32
programme.desc: Franco asks Miguel to keep an eye on Mia. Roberta's going out with Joaquín but she lies about her parents. Pilar confronts her mom for cheating.

programme.title: Capítulo 33
programme.desc: Joaquín asks Roberta to be discreet about them. Diego has Roberta do his history assignment. Miguel get the Lodge to think the old Lodge is back.

programme.title: Capítulo 34
programme.desc: Joaquín rejects Mia in front of Miguel and convinces her to keep their relationship secret. Miguel learns that Joaquín is playing with both Roberta and Mia.

programme.title: Capítulo 35
programme.desc: Roberta rips into Diego for spreading gossip and tells him she's going to send the tape with his name to all the radio stations as pay back.

programme.title: Capítulo 36
programme.desc: Marcelino apologizes to Roberta for thinking she gave him up. Mia won't accept Celina's apology. Roberta finds out Diego didn't tell on her.

programme.title: Capítulo 37
programme.desc: León finds out about the tape and slaps Diego, but Tomás takes the blame. Celina tells Mia about Joaquín and Roberta. A drunken Diego hits his Dad.

programme.title: Capítulo 38
programme.desc: Mia buys Celina a dance outfit. Joaquín finds out Alma is Roberta's mom. Vico tells Mia she'll lose Joaquín if she doesn't sleep with him.

programme.title: Frida
programme.desc: The most prominent female painter of Latin America, Frida Kahlo, is agonizing in her Coyoacán home. She evokes memories of her childhood, of the streetcar accident that caused her terrible pain and affliction, her friendship with Trotsky and painter Alfaro Siqueiros, her marriage to Diego Rivera, her miscarriage, her political commitment, her love affairs and the anticipated exhibition of her works.

programme.title: La venganza de la maestra
programme.desc: Alejandra descubre que la maestra de Morris es Belén, la misma maestra que ella molestaba. Chelo, la empleada doméstica de Silvia, tiene más suerte en el amor que ella.

programme.title: Arturo amuleto
programme.desc: Jorjáis cree que Arturo le trae buena suerte a su equipo de fútbol cuando va a los partidos, por lo que buscará la manera para que él esté presente en la final del torneo y así poder ganar.

programme.title: La bomba de agua
programme.desc: Los vecinos le piden a Germán que repare la bomba de agua del edificio para evitar pagarle a un plomero profesional. Jovita intenta que Pedro cambie sus hábitos alimenticios, pero él se rehúsa y hace una huelga de hambre.

programme.title: Echando cohetes
programme.desc: Los vecinos se molestan por el ruido que provocan los cohetes de una fiesta. Rocko se queja de que Alejandra no es cariñosa con él.

programme.title: Un día de vida
programme.desc: En el laboratorio de diagnóstico le dicen a Ludovico que a Federica le quedan pocas horas de vida y para darle más tiempo, evitaría un disgusto. Por su parte, Federica lee en una revista que si un esposo trata muy bien a su mujer significa que planea abandonarle.

programme.title: Buenos padres
programme.desc: Gracias a Ludovico, los padres de un compañero de Ludoviquito hacen una visita a su casa para conocerlos, por lo que los P.Luche deben actuar más decentemente para impresionarlos.

programme.title: Reality show
programme.desc: Un productor descubre a los P. Luche (incluyendo Excelsa) y los invita a participar en un programa similar a Big Brother, el cual se realizaría en la propia casa de la familia con muchas cámaras vigilándolos, y el ganador recibirá un millón de pesos.

programme.title: Ahorros de Navidad
programme.desc: Ludovico se gastó los ahorros para las compras navideñas, por lo mismo debe pensar rápido cómo recuperar ese dinero y no dejar sin regalos a su familia.

programme.title: El verdadero padre de Jr.
programme.desc: Un socio de Don Camerino resulta ser el papá de Junior y ahora deben de ocultarlo.

programme.title: La familia P. Luche
programme.desc: Follows a dysfunctional family in Peluche City.

programme.title: San Chambelán
programme.desc: Maria has her Quinceañera party meanwhile Doña Lucha rents out her son's services as chamberlain.

programme.title: Sostengo el Pi
programme.desc: María de todos los Ángeles, ve atentamente un programa de televisión en el que el maestro de yoga da tres consejos básicos del feng shui para atraer la fortuna y el amor, esto provoca que María inmediatamente se ponga a mover muebles y a tirar cosas, al ver esto Carmelito pone el grito en el cielo, hasta que su nieta lo convence de que con esto sencillos cambios podrán atraer la fortuna y el dinero. Delfino y Betsabé llegan a casa de Doña Lucha a cobrar el dinero de una tanda que les debe de tiempo atrás, ésta que también ha visto el programa, y no tiene para pagarles, usa los argumentos de maestro Chuy y los convence de que se inscriban en un curso de feng shui que va a impartir, con lo cual aprovecha para ponerlos a mover los muebles de su casa, hasta que ya cansados deciden abandonar el curso. Como segundo paso Doña Lucha decide escombrar la casa y comienza a tirar cosas de Rosa Aurora, Albertano y del Chino, pero el mono de coco que ella trajo de Acapulco lo vuelve a colocar en el mismo lugar. La euforia por limpiar lleva a las dos familias hasta la azotea en la que se desata un conflicto por el espacio y por el respeto a sus pertenencias.

programme.title: Número equivocado
programme.desc: Alguien grafiteó la fachada del edificio. Para retirarlo, Germán cambia el número de la entrada. ¿Recibirá Luis el paquete que espera? ¿Acudirán a pintar el departamento de Lorena?

programme.title: Magdalena y la tercera edad
programme.desc: Magdalena descubre que las personas de tercera edad recibirán tarjetas de ayuda. La familia López Pérez intenta obtener el beneficio. A Luis le molesta que Benito tenga un auto nuevo.

programme.title: Cásate conmigo
programme.desc: Comienza una guerra tras la pelea entre Silvia y Luis, sin embargo él le pide matrimonio. Lorena le pide a Germán que arregle una mesa.

programme.title: La boda
programme.desc: Luis y Silvia anuncian su boda y los vecinos los convencerán para que se casen en el edificio y ayudarán a los preparativos.

programme.title: Vecinos ruidosos
programme.desc: Los vecinos se quejan de los ruidos que vienen del departamento de Luis y Silvita, Germán intenta reparar la tubería de Lorena y los López Pérez sufren de comezón.

programme.title: Frankie regresa
programme.desc: Frankie regresa a vivir al edificio, con Lorena y con Benito, pero con la condición de que encuentre trabajo. Pero, en vez de eso, escribirá una nueva película.

programme.title: La llegada de Vanesa
programme.desc: Vanesa vuelve al edificio para visitar a los Vecinos y ponerse al corriente. Benito invita a su novia a "ver pelis" en el departamento, al enterarse de que sus papás saldrán.

programme.title: La cocina de Silvita
programme.desc: Como Luis le ha preparado el desayuno, Silvita se encargará de prepararle a su esposo un platillo espectacular.

programme.title: Vecinos
programme.desc: In a neighborhood in Mexico City, the neighbors have problems and silly misadventures with one another.

programme.title: La cosas de Don Roque
programme.desc: Germán le entrega a Vanessa cosas que le dejó don Roque, por lo que ambos lo recuerdan y homenajean. Lorena aprecia a Soledad, la nueva amiga de Benito.

programme.title: El tamalero
programme.desc: Como el tamalero no deja dormir a Luis, este le pedirá que le baje a su bocina. Ve cuántos enemigos se echará encima, por dejar sin trabajo al tamalero.

programme.title: Los paramédicos
programme.desc: Por pedirle a Morris que ponga en su tarea que son paramédicos, Rocko y Ale deben ir a la escuela a dar un curso de primeros auxilios. Llegan nuevos vecinos al departamento 301.

programme.title: Vecinos
programme.desc: In a neighborhood in Mexico City, the neighbors have problems and silly misadventures with one another.

programme.title: Los ex
programme.desc: Luis le pide a Silvita que deje de hablar con su ex, pues se siente incómodo, sin sospechar que Maru, ex de Luis, llegará a la puerta de su casa. Además, la obra de arte de Jorjais.

programme.title: El cumpleaños de Germán
programme.desc: Todos se olvidan del cumpleaños de Germán, a pesar de estar invitados a la fiesta. Lorena y Frankie le dicen a Benito que volverá a los castings.

programme.title: La amiga incómoda
programme.desc: La amiga de Silvita se queda en el departamento de los recién casados, ya que se ha quedado sin casa. Además, Magda trata de convencer a los vecinos para que acepten poner un anuncio en la fachada del edificio.

programme.title: El nuevo reglamento
programme.desc: Los Rivers solo tienen dinero para comer atún. Así que Frankie envía a Benito a hacer el casting para interpretar al Sol. Pero Luis propone realizar un nuevo reglamento de convivencia vecinal.

programme.title: La historia de Jorjáis
programme.desc: Jorjáis cuenta su verdadera historia, y hará llorar a más de uno. Mientras tanto, como Frankie y Lorena no están en casa, Benito y Liz aprovecharán para hacer algo en su ausencia.

programme.title: Sortilegio
programme.desc: Alejandro returns home after a terrible accident to discover that he is married to Maria Jose, a poor, innocent girl who was deceived by Alejandro's brother Bruno. Bruno used Alejandro's name to marry Maria Jose, soon after he sent to kill his brother in hopes that Maria Jose would inherit Alejandro's fortune. But when Maria Jose arrives at the mansion she realizes that Bruno lied to her and used her. She is also scared when Alejandro is found alive and returns home. Doctors tell Alejandro that he suffers from memory loss but he is convinced he never met Maria Jose before and goes in search of the truth, but during this time and Maria Jose actually fall in love, while Bruno and Maura (Alejandro's ex-girlfriend) plot to break them up. Meanwhile, the brothers' mother Victoria has fallen in love with Fernando--Alejandro's best friend, so closer to his age than hers.

programme.title: David Pollack Primetime
programme.desc: David Pollack Primetime delivers a fact-driven breakdown of the day's biggest stories, going beyond the headlines to explain what's really happening and why it matters.

programme.title: Runaway Country with Alex Wagner (Election Chaos: Texas Republicans vs California Dems)
programme.desc: Could reality tv star Spencer Pratt become Mayor of Los Angeles? And could Trump’s endorsement of Ken Paxton cause Texas to go blue? This week, Alex dives into all things primaries to see if we can read the tea leaves for what awaits both Republicans and Democrats in the future. She’s joined by Nikki Laurenzo, anchor and political reporter for FOX40 in Sacramento, to break down the chaotic and still very undecided races shaping California politics. Then she speaks to Crooked Media’s Dan Pfeifffer, co-host of Pod Save America and author of the Message Box newsletter, to get a read on the consequences of Senator John Cornyn’s loss to Ken Paxton, and why a crowded gubernatorial primary in California could be a warning for Democratic presidential hopefuls in 2028.

programme.title: Hysteria (Welcome to the Jungle Primary w. Reshma Saujani)
programme.desc: Erin and Alyssa cover Trump’s nemesis Thomas Massie’s loss in Kentucky, a positive (for now) Mifepristone update, the ridiculous Garden of Heroes, and what the hell is going on in California politics. Then, Reshma Saujani joins to discuss her new documentary No Country for Mothers, and the real issues moms face. They wrap up in the sanity corner with rave reviews of Cookie Queens and Widow's Bay.

programme.title: What a Day (ICE Is Still Detaining People)
programme.desc:
Trump's immigration policies haven't been getting much attention lately. But don't be fooled — the immigration detention industrial complex hasn't slowed down. One infamous facility in Florida, known as "Alligator Alcatraz," is reportedly closing. Katie Blankenship is an attorney representing immigrants across the country – including some who have been detained at "Alligator Alcatraz". Jane talked about what she's hearing from her clients and what you need to know about the immigrant experience in America right now.
And in headlines, former First Lady Jill Biden admits she thought President Joe Biden was having a stroke during that debate, Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent thinks it's totally normal to put Trump's face on a 250-dollar bill, and Kentucky Republican Rep. Thomas Massie and former Georgia Rep. Marjorie Taylor Greene vacation in Costa Rica with their partners.

programme.title: Lovett or Leave It (Look on Trump's works, ye Mighty, and despair!)
programme.desc: The president makes waves with his Reflecting Pool reno, the U.S. treads water on the Iran peace process, and Trump’s crypto golden calf is more than a little fishy. This week, rock legend Melissa Etheridge lets the lesbian good times roll. Speaking of roll, Bosco brings the magic while we rank the sexy monsters of Magic: The Gathering. And to close us out, Lovett serves up a piping hot round of Second Thoughts.

programme.title: Offline with Jon Favreau (Healing Our Broken Brains)
programme.desc: Cal Newport, computer scientist and author of Deep Work, joins Offline to explain why we need a revolution in cognitive fitness, and how AI is going to get in the way. Cal and Jon explore how smartphones and AI are destroying our ability to concentrate, how our attention spans are a third of what they were just twenty years ago, and how we can practice “deep work” in our constantly interrupted digital environment.

programme.title: Pod Save America (Trump Admits He's for He/Him, Not You)
programme.desc: Republicans attempt to defend President Trump's admission that he does not "think about Americans' financial situations," while his Department of Justice is on the verge of giving him billions of taxpayer dollars to settle the lawsuit he brought against his own IRS. Jon and Dan react to the president's financial priorities and then turn to Democratic strategy, including how they should be talking about the administration's corruption, how they're fighting back against Republican gerrymandering, and the latest on the DNC's refusal to release its 2024 autopsy.

programme.title: Keep It (Jane Fonda’s Favorite Husband)
programme.desc: Louis is joined by writers Gus Hickey and Chris Schleicher to discuss the latest edition of Eurovision and the finale of The Comeback. Then they dive into the legacy of Ted Turner and share their favorite classic films listeners should add to their watchlists. Plus, actor Taylour Paige joins to talk about her new movie I Love Boosters.

programme.title: The Rutabaga Story
programme.desc: There's a problem in Hooterville: no farmer seems able to grow a cash crop on account of the quality of the soil. But as it turns out from Oliver's (Eddie Albert) soil and mineral research, the oft misunderstood rutabaga is an ideal choice.

programme.title: A Star Named Arnold Is Born (Part 1)
programme.desc: Green Acres goes Hollywood in this first installment of a two-part season finale. When Arnold the pig appears in a play at the Hooterville Theater, Lisa (Eva Gabor) arranges for an old friend to give Arnold a chance on the big screen.

programme.title: A Star Named Arnold Is Born (Part 2)
programme.desc: It's a swine-sational third season finale for Green Acres, as Oliver and Lisa chaperone Arnold, the Ziffel's talented pig, to Hollywood for his big screen test. There's only one problem, Lisa failed to mention that Arnold is not your normal actor.

programme.title: Guess Who's Not Going To The Luau
programme.desc: Hooterville is abuzz as the townsfolk enter a contest sponsored by Peterson's Poi Juice, with a trip to Hawaii as the top prize. But no one expects the winner to be Arnold, the Ziffels' talented pig. Maybe a pig might fly after all, to the islands!

programme.title: The Rummage Sale
programme.desc: A charity rummage sale asks for donated clothing. Being among the wealthier residents of Hooterville, the organizers look to the Douglases. It isn't long before Oliver has to stop Lisa from giving away his entire wardrobe!

programme.title: Eb's Romance
programme.desc: Love is in the air when Eb is quickly engaged to Loreli Appleby, whom he has only known for a day. Eb tries to impress Loreli's father, a farmer, but in typical fashion, he accidentally ruins his future father-in-law's cabbage crop.

programme.title: Hail To The Fire Chief
programme.desc: When Fire Chief Joe Carson is looking for a way to go to Miami, he hires Oliver as his assistant, hoping Oliver will pay for the trip. The plan backfires, no pun intended, when Oliver starts pointing out what a poor job the chief has been doing.

programme.title: The Candidate
programme.desc: The highways in Hooterville are in dire need of repair. Oliver is fed up and believes he can make a difference by complaining to his state representative, Ben Hanks. But Hanks' popularity proves greater than his sense of responsibility.

programme.title: Handy Lessons
programme.desc: Lisa feels like she should be contributing more and wants to start working in Oliver's construction business. Lisa is like a fish out of water as she arrives to the Monroes' workshop in overalls and causes one problem after another.

programme.title: A Husband For Eleanor
programme.desc: Lisa plays bovine matchmaker when Oliver and Lisa decide it is time for their female cow, Eleanor, to start providing milk for the farm. Oliver makes arrangements for Eleanor to mooooove in with the neighbor's bull, but Lisa has other ideas.

programme.title: Old Mail Day
programme.desc: The townsfolk look forward to "Old Mail Day", when once a year undelivered mail is handed out. The delayed mail includes a letter all the way from 1917! Oliver is upset by the poor service, but his complaints land with more impact than he intended.

programme.title: The Agricultural Student
programme.desc: When word spreads that an agricultural student is coming to Hooterville to study with county agent Hank Kimball, no one seems to be interested. However, things change when the folks discover that the student is an attractive blonde.

programme.title: How Hooterville Was Floundered
programme.desc: This episode tells the unusual tale of Hooterville's founding. As the town prepares for its centennial, Oliver announces a reenactment of the town's origins and casts himself and Lisa in the roles of Horace and Doris Hooter.

programme.title: The Blue Feather
programme.desc: When a blue feather arrives in the mail for Lisa, her Hungarian roots tell her that trouble is brewing. The feather is a symbol of malfortune to come, and she suspects it was sent by Hungarian gypsies, a warning that they are out to get her.

programme.title: Everywhere A Chick Chick
programme.desc: Don't count chickens before they hatch! Oliver thinks he's laid a gold egg of an idea when he decides to dive beak first into the business of raising chickens. Lisa, on the other hand, sees little difference between raising chickens and children.

programme.title: A Prize In Each And Every Package
programme.desc: All that glitters is not frosted cereal. After a jewelry heist in Chicago, the robbers foolishly stash their booty in the grain bin of a cereal manufacturer. Oliver and Lisa discover the jewels in their bowl of Crickly Wickly!

programme.title: Law Partners

programme.title: Gomez, The Politician
programme.desc: Election fever grips the Addams household, as Gomez (John Astin) supports the council candidate whose campaign promise is to drain the town's disgusting bogs. Morticia (Carolyn Jones), who likes "the lovely bogs," labels the candidate an extremist.

programme.title: The Addams Family Tree
programme.desc: Pugsley and Wednesday start a row at a party when their gift of a tarantula upsets Harold, the birthday boy. Harold's parents insist that the Addams children lack breeding and, therefore, are no longer allowed to play with their son.

programme.title: Morticia Joins The Ladies League
programme.desc: After Pugsley befriends Gorgo, a circus gorilla, Gorgo decides to follow Pugsley and Gomez home, where he is accepted as one of the family. But Gorgo gives Morticia's tea guests, the Ladies League members, quite a shock.

programme.title: Halloween With The Addams Family
programme.desc: A pair of bank robbers are welcomed as Halloween trick-or-treaters by Morticia and Gomez. The creepy atmosphere of the house, Morticia's smoldering holiday punch and Lurch's ominous presence impel the crooks to abandon their plans.

programme.title: Green Eyed Gomez
programme.desc: When Lionel Barker, a former suitor of Morticia, shows up, Gomez is jealous. But he's soon calmed when he learns that Barker is after Mildred, the new maid. The Addamses then discover that Barker is a con man--after Mildred's money, not her love.

programme.title: New Neighbors Meet The Addams Family
programme.desc: Newlyweds move in next door. But the honeymoon loses its tranquility when Uncle Fester enters their house through a tunnel and they flee in terror at the sight of Lurch, Grandmama sharpening her ax, and Morticia and Gomez bathing the octopus.

programme.title: Wednesday Leaves Home
programme.desc: The family faces a crisis when Wednesday packs her spider and runs away (after being scolded for playing with Uncle Fester's TNT). When they try to file a missing person report, a detective is anxious to retire early due to their story.

programme.title: The Addams Family Meets The V.I.P.'s
programme.desc: Official visitors from an unfriendly nation receive the shock of their lives when they decide to exploit the Addams Family as an average uncultured family. The visitors are shaken up by Morticia's carnivorous plants among other things.

programme.title: Morticia, The Matchmaker
programme.desc: Morticia's cousin, Melancholia, wants to marry Clarence P. Harvey after most of her marital prospects have joined the Foreign Legion. Clarence recoils when Melancholia puts on a campaign to win him, backed by Morticia, Gomez and Clarence's boss.

programme.title: Lurch Learns To Dance
programme.desc: Lurch becomes a veritable gazelle when he takes dancing lessons at Gomez and Morticia's suggestion, after he admits to being a wallflower at social functions. Each member of the Addams Family teaches Lurch a different step.

programme.title: Art And The Addams Family
programme.desc: Grandmama's paintings leave an art critic speechless, so Gomez sends for his friend, Sam Picasso, to teach Grandmama to paint in the abstract. The starving artist ignores Grandmama's and Morticia's work as he eats and paints.

programme.title: The Addams Family Meets A Beatnik
programme.desc: Rockland Cartright III is a beatnik who crashes his motorbike into a tree and recuperates at the Addams house. Each regards the other as an oddity, although the family learns to appreciate certain aspects of Rockland's life.

programme.title: The Addams Family Meets The Undercover Man
programme.desc: Mr. Briggs, the postman, is hired by the government to do undercover work in the Addams house. While pretending to be a stamp collector, he investigates rumors that coded radio messages are being sent from the house.

programme.title: Mother Lurch Visits The Addams Family
programme.desc: Lurch's tiny fire-eating mother visits and demands that Lurch's employers wait on her boy hand-and-foot after Lurch leads her to believe that it's his house. Lurch revels in the unexpected attention he gets from Morticia and Gomez.

programme.title: Uncle Fester's Illness
programme.desc: Uncle Fester's electrical output fails abruptly. Should they call a doctor or an electrician to bring Fester back? Morticia prescribes a bowl of porcupine mold, and both she an Gomez agree that long exposure to moon rays would be beneficial.

programme.title: The Addams Family Splurges
programme.desc: The family would like to vacation on the moon, but the billion dollar travel cost is slightly prohibitive. Uncle Fester volunteers $21.13 and Morticia, using Gomez's new supercomputer, suggests placing a $2,000 bet on the daily double horse race.

programme.title: Cousin Itt Visits The Addams Family
programme.desc: Cousin Itt is advocated as curator of the local zoo. Gomez uses his influence with Park Commissioner Fisk (guest star Alan Reed) to promote Itt for the job, but the zookeepers are convinced the Addamses' cousin should be confined to a cage.

programme.title: This Might Be My God Moment
programme.desc: Are you standing over a situation that's too big for you? If you feel like your hope, logic, and options are at a dead end, pay attention because this might be your God moment.

programme.title: All Those Times You Didnt See
programme.desc: The efforts you make when no one's watching are the ones God uses to shape you. Just because you don't see the outcome yet doesn't mean He's ignoring you; He's positioning you. Keep showing up while you wait.

programme.title: All You've Got To Do Is Don't
programme.desc: We all find ourselves in seasons when we feel like we don't have what it takes, and we don't know what to do. But don't be afraid or discouraged, because God isn't late - He's working according to His timing. Don't lose hope, and don't give up.

programme.title: The God Of Pieces
programme.desc: The devil wants to keep you overwhelmed by your situation, but God wants to show you how to deal with it piece by piece. And as you learn to praise God for the piece you have, you'll experience the peace you've been praying for.

programme.title: I Thought It Was Through
programme.desc: When despair sets in, it's easy to assume it'll always be this way. But what if a shift in thinking could break that cycle? Your thoughts have the power to disrupt your despair.

programme.title: When God Gives A Thought To You
programme.desc: We live in a world that values things more than thoughts, so when God speaks to us, we don't always value it like we should. In this message, learn some of the ways God speaks, when to ask for it, and how to recognize it when He does.

programme.title: The Hardest Part Is Behind You
programme.desc: Don't judge any one part of your life in isolation or let unmet expectations define your future. God's not finished with your story, and He didn't bring you this far to let you turn back now.

programme.title: Not As Long As My Song!
programme.desc: Hitting your limit isn't a sign of weakness; it's an invitation to shift your perspective. When the enemy brings the battle to your mind, remember that he only attacks what's valuable. And God's goodness is your secret weapon to win the fight.

programme.title: Hand Him The Plan
programme.desc: Sometimes the battle in your mind is proof that God's trying to do something great in your heart. What you see isn't the full picture of what God has for you. When you hand God your plans, you make room for His purpose.

programme.title: How to Defeat Your Stress, Anxiety & Inaction
programme.desc: If motivation can’t be trusted, what should you rely on instead? Bestselling author and top podcaster Mel Robbins explains why discipline and emotional control are the real keys to success, and how to build the mindset needed to follow through when it counts. Expect to learn why we cling to control even when we know it makes us miserable and why we focus on things we can't control, how to better deal with the judgment of others, where self-compassion comes from, how to get a grip on your anxiety and ADHD, what ""Let Them"" theory means and how to employ it, where self-compassion actually comes from and much more...

programme.title: The Personal Pain of Country Music
programme.desc: Odds are, your favorite country song wasn’t written by the person singing it; it was probably written by HARDY (or someone in his writing circle, maybe). So how did a Mississippi country boy become one of Nashville’s most powerful behind-the-scenes writers and producers? Expect to learn what HARDY’s philosophy for his career and life is, what most people do not understand about how the Nashville scene works, how to balance the unique tension between being a songwriter and an artist, the story of the bus crash that almost killed HARDY, how to use vulnerability and turn it into a strength and much more…

programme.title: Uncertain About Your Relationship? You Need This…
programme.desc: How do you know if you’re with the right person — or if you're just telling yourself you are? If you’re questioning the relationship more than enjoying it, Matthew Hussey might have the clarity you need. So what does finding your person actually look like, and what signs should you pay attention to? Expect to learn how to know when it’s time to leave a relationship, the 5 questions to ask yourself if you’re unsure about your relationship, why people struggle so hard with letting others go, how women can best support their man and visa versa, how important attractiveness is to both men and women in a successful relationship, ways to learnt to be more vulnerable and much more…

programme.title: Living with Confidence & Going All In
programme.desc: Expect to learn why Matthew says life often “rhymes”, why it’s okay if you do everything right and still not get the result you want, how Matthew views the role courage plays in his life, how to learn to deal with failure, the difference between a nice guy and a good man, the most important principle Matthew refuses to compromise on, the appropriate balance of running toward a life you want vs. away from one that you fear, how to balance self-confidence with humility, the difference between “being” and “acting”, what it means to be more impressed with the wow instead of the how, rumors on Matthew’s return to the True Detective franchise, and much more…
